title: delivery.rs: inbox_uri parsed twice per delivery (domain + path)
description:
LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:174 (extract_domain call) and 270-272 (second url::Url::parse for path)

FINDING: For each delivery item, process_batch calls extract_domain(&inbox_uri) which internally calls url::Url::parse(&inbox_uri), then later calls url::Url::parse(&inbox_uri) again at line 270-272 to extract the path for signing. Two full URL parses per delivery item, each allocating heap memory for the parsed structure and the resulting String.

These two parses can be trivially merged: parse once, extract both .host_str() and .path() in one call.

IMPACT: 2x URL parse allocations per delivery item. With a batch of 50 items, that is 100 URL parses instead of 50. Each parse allocates; the combined cost is measurable though still dominated by the HTTP round-trip.
